plugins.name: ready-to-archive
plugins.description: A last-gate safety check before you archive a session. Run /ready-to-archive or ask 'can I archive this?' and it audits the session against three pillars — RECORDED (every decision, conclusion, and change is written where the project keeps it, not only in this chat), COMMITTED (the git tree is clean and pushed if it must live remotely), and LANDED (branch work is merged to main where it had to be, or the open PR is named) — verifying each against git and the filesystem, not the chat recollection. Defaults to caution: anything it can't verify is a gap, never waved through. Ends with one clear GO / NO-GO verdict and offers to close any gaps — but never commits, pushes, merges, or records on your behalf without an explicit go-ahead.
